summary: A breathtaking audience recording that captures the Wall of Sound in stunning 3‑D stereo, documenting a fire‑filled 1974 show that still feels alive.
review: The Bertrando tape is hailed as an almost perfect audience capture of the legendary Wall of Sound, offering extraordinary depth, clarity and frequency range for a 1974 concert. Listeners who were there (or imagine being) describe the band as "on fire" with a relaxed two‑set flow that makes this release a must‑listen despite its unknown date.

ai_rating.rationale: All six reviewers are overwhelmingly enthusiastic about both the performance and the tape quality; recording praise outweighs any minor criticism, yielding a top‑tier rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note 3‑D stereo imaging, superb depth and frequency range, and a clean capture that rivals soundboard tapes; ambient chatter is present but not distracting.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Two‑set concert with a relaxed vibe; reviewers highlight the “sweet space‑out” around the 7‑minute mark and overall high energy throughout.
